ALL SPECIALTIES / SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGIST / COLORADO

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Colorado Speech-Language Pathologist License

Colorado Speech-Language Pathologist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Speech-Language Pathologist applications in Colorado run through Division of Professions and Occupations — Speech-Language Pathology. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official graduate transcripts - From a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score report - Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verification - CF completion documentation.
  • Background check - Criminal background check via CBI.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Colorado is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Colorado through a compact privilege.

  1. STEP 1

    Create an Account on the DORA Applicant Portal

    Access the DORA Applicant Portal at https://www.colorado.gov/dora/licensing/applicant-login.action and select 'Register a New User' to establish your online account with the Division of Professions and Occupations.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Initiate Your Online Application

    Log into your DORA Applicant Portal account, click on 'Application' from the main menu, and then select 'Audiologist & Speech-Language Pathologist - Speech-Language Pathologist' to begin filling out the licensing application.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Required Documentation and Praxis Scores

    Complete all sections of the online application, including personal, educational, and examination history. Upload official transcripts verifying a master's or doctoral degree in SLP from an accredited institution and proof of successful completion of the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Verify Supervised Post-Graduate Experience (CFY)

    Ensure your supervisor submits the necessary documentation to the Board, verifying your successful completion of 1,260 hours of supervised post-graduate professional experience (Clinical Fellowship Year or equivalent). The online application will provide guidance on this submission process.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay the Application Fee

    Submit the required application fee of $140 through the DORA Applicant Portal using a valid credit or debit card to finalize your application submission.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Monitor Application Status

    Regularly log into your DORA Applicant Portal account to track the status of your application, review any deficiencies, and respond promptly to requests for additional information from the Office of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology Licensure.

    Fee: $120 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$120
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$120

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Division of Professions and Occupations — Speech-Language Pathology
Phone
303-894-7800
Website
dpo.colorado.gov/SpeechLanguagePathology
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Speech-Language Pathologist renewal in Colorado follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$128
Continuing education
30 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Colorado SLP license cost?

The initial application and license fee is $120. Biennial renewal is $75.

Is Colorado part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Colorado has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

Does Colorado allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Colorado permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

Does Colorado have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

Yes. Colorado participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Colorado without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Colorado?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Colorado while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$120

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years · $128

Licensing board

Division of Professions and Occupations — Speech-Language Pathology

dpo.colorado.gov/SpeechLanguagePathology

303-894-7800

This page is maintained as a reference for speech-language pathologists and recruiters evaluating Colorado assignments.
